text: Three alternative representations for non-negative integers, n, are:

list_items:  Peano: values have the form S(... S(Z) ...), applying S n times to Z where S and Z are constructors or constants of some data type.
 Binary: values are of type bool list with 0 being represented as the empty list, and the least-significant bit being stored in the head of the list.
 Church: values have the form fn f => fn x => f(... f(x) ...), applying f n times to x
(a) Write ML functions for each of these data types which take the representation of an integer n as argument and return n as an ML int. [6 marks]

(b) Write ML functions for each of these data types which take representations of integers m and n and return the representation of m + n. Your answers must not use any value or operation on type int or real. [Hint: you might it useful to write a function majority: bool*bool*bool -> bool (which returns true when two or more of its arguments are true) and to note that the ML inequality operator ‘<>’ acts as exclusive-or on bool.] [10 marks]

(c) Letting two and three respectively be the Church representations of integers 2 and 3, indicate whether each of the following ML expressions give a Church representation of some integer and, if so what integer is represented, and if not giving a one-line reason.

text: COMPUTER SCIENCE TRIPOS Part IA – 2019 – Paper 1
